problem.display_name: Module 1 Quiz — What Is Physical AI?

multiplechoiceresponse.label: What is the fundamental difference between classical robotics and Physical AI?

choicegroup.choice: Classical robotics uses more sensors than Physical AI
Classical robotics is programmed with explicit rules; Physical AI learns from data
Physical AI only works in simulation, not on real robots
Classical robotics is faster than Physical AI

solution.p: Physical AI replaces hand-coded rules with learned policies trained on demonstration data. This makes it more adaptable but requires high-quality training data.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: What does a VLA model output that a VLM does not?

choicegroup.choice: Robot actions (joint commands or end-effector movements)

solution.p: VLA = Vision-Language-Action. The "Action" part is what distinguishes it from a VLM. A VLA takes image + language instruction and outputs robot actions directly.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: Why is data considered the primary bottleneck in Physical AI?

choicegroup.choice: There are no good simulation environments
Robot training data must be physically embodied, synchronized, task-specific, and consistent — making it hard to collect at scale
AI models for robotics don't exist yet

solution.p: Unlike internet text or images, robot data requires physical embodiment, precise temporal synchronization, and task-specific demonstrations. This makes it expensive and slow to collect.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: What is the correct order of the Physical AI loop?

choicegroup.choice: Teleop → Dataset → Train Policy → Evaluate → Improve → Deploy

solution.p: The Physical AI loop always starts with data collection (teleop), then training, then evaluation, then improvement. Deployment comes after the policy meets performance requirements.

multiplechoiceresponse.label: Which of these is an example of a VLA model?

solution.p: OpenVLA and π0 are Vision-Language-Action models — they take visual input and language instructions and output robot actions. GPT-4 is a language model, Stable Diffusion generates images, YOLO detects objects.
